root_text.an4.44:1.3: Kodhagarutā na saddhammagarutā, makkhagarutā na saddhammagarutā, lābhagarutā na saddhammagarutā, sakkāragarutā na saddhammagarutā.
root_text.an4.44:2.3: Saddhammagarutā na kodhagarutā, saddhammagarutā na makkhagarutā, saddhammagarutā na lābhagarutā, saddhammagarutā na sakkāragarutā.

translation_text.an4.44:1.1: “Mendicants, these four things oppose the true teaching.
translation_text.an4.44:1.3: Valuing anger, denigration, material things, and honor rather than the true teaching.
translation_text.an4.44:1.4: These are the four things that oppose the true teaching.
translation_text.an4.44:2.1: These four things are the true teaching.
translation_text.an4.44:2.3: Valuing the true teaching rather than anger, denigration, material things, and honor.
translation_text.an4.44:2.4: These are the four things that are the true teaching.
translation_text.an4.44:3.1: A mendicant who values anger and denigration,
translation_text.an4.44:3.3: doesn’t grow in the true teaching,
translation_text.an4.44:3.4: like a rotten seed in a good field.
translation_text.an4.44:4.1: But those who value the true teaching,
translation_text.an4.44:4.2: who have lived it, and are living it now,
translation_text.an4.44:4.3: these do grow in the teaching,
